text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Hey, Dia, you're really into that book,<n>huh?
What's it about?

text_block_list.name: Satono Diamond
text_block_list.text: It's called The Encyclopedia of World Superstitions. It's all about how thesmallest things you do—or don't do—canbring about various jinxes.

text_block_list.name: Satono Diamond
text_block_list.text:
Hee hee. With so many jinxes, it's fun to<n>plan out
how I'll overcome each and<n>every one of them! ♪

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Haha, you're always taking on new<n>challenges,
huh? You must really love it!

text_block_list.name: Satono Diamond
text_block_list.text:
This one looks particularly intriguing. "If<n>two
people attempt to meet up at a<n>festival, one will
always get lost<n>somehow."

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Whooa! Festivals are my thing! We<n>should try it
out next time!

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Wait, we've been to lots of festivals<n>together, but
aren't you always getting<n>lost even without the
jinx?!

text_block_list.name: Satono Diamond
text_block_list.text:
Oh? You might be right... Hehe, and<n>when I think
about it, there might not be<n>any need for us to
overcome this one,<n>anyway.

text_block_list.name: Satono Diamond
text_block_list.text: Because, Kita, even if we do get splitup, you always manage to find me. ♪
